description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a classic American architectural style, featuring a prominent front porch supported by columns and a set of steps leading up to the main entrance. The exterior is clad in horizontal siding painted a muted gray-blue, with white trim accentuating the windows, doors, and porch railings. The roof is a simple gable type with two red brick chimneys and a slight overhang. The front facade includes a bay window on the second floor and decorative stonework above the porch. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front and sides, with a mix of rectangular and bay styles. The house sits on a red brick foundation and has a covered side porch extending along one side.
description: A two-story gray-blue house with a gable roof, front porch, bay window, and red brick foundation. Features white trim, symmetrical windows, and two chimneys.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with steps, covered porch, bay window above, and decorative stonework.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the extended covered porch, multiple windows, and the length of the gable roof with two chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back porch, secondary entry, and simpler window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with symmetrical window placement and clear view of the brick foundation and porch railing.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with steps, covered porch, columns, and decorative stonework
